summary: No-ship: the validator can return `Done` for incomplete/ambiguous gate metadata and can also return `Done` after failing to persist the required `validated` marker.

findings.title: `codexPwTest: "n/a"` is accepted for UI-capable slices, allowing `Done` without a passing Playwright check
findings.recommendation: Require `codexPwTest.result === "pass"` for UI slices, and only tolerate `"n/a"` for explicitly non-UI kinds. Add a regression test covering `kind: "ui"` with `codexPwTest.result: "n/a"` returning `Implement`.

findings.title: Missing or unrecognized `kind` defaults to `automation`, so partial/garbage `gate-status.json` can skip UI-only gates and still yield `Done`
findings.recommendation: Validate `kind` strictly against the allowed enum and fail closed when it is missing or invalid. Do not infer `automation` from absent metadata if the goal is to prevent partial/garbage gate files from producing `Done`. Add tests for missing and garbage `kind` values.

findings.title: A failed marker write is non-fatal, so the command can print `Done` even though the durable `validated` record was never persisted
findings.recommendation: Treat failure to write `validated` as a validation failure for routing purposes, or at minimum make `--strict` and the normal verdict fail closed when the required durable marker cannot be persisted. Add a test that simulates write/rename failure and asserts the final line is `Implement`.
